channel.title: Fall 2021 on Sibelius Peng
channel.description: Recent content in Fall 2021 on Sibelius Peng

item.title: AMATH 343
item.description: This course introduces discrete mathematical models used in applied mathematics. Topics include radioactive decay models, population growth dynamics, and their applications. Note: These notes are incomplete and cover only introductory material.

item.title: AMATH 453
item.description:
The notes is incomplete. But you can refer to krmatthe.

These notes cover advanced topics in partial differential equations. Topics include the wave equation, conservation laws, the diffusion equation, maximum principle, uniqueness of solutions, diffusion and wave reflections on half-lines, and problems with source terms.

item.title: CO 370
item.description: This course focuses on deterministic operations research models. Topics include linear programming formulations (production problems, minimax), network flow models (max flow, min cost flow), integer programming formulations and tricks, cone programming (SOC programs), and robust optimization under uncertainty.

item.title: ECON 212
item.description: This course provides an introduction to game theory. Topics include strategic games (Prisoner’s Dilemma, Battle of the Sexes, Matching Pennies, Stag Hunt) and Nash equilibrium in pure strategies. Note: These notes are incomplete.

item.title: PMATH 333
item.description: This course covers the foundations of real analysis. Topics include axioms of the real number system, topology of Rⁿ (open/closed sets, connectedness, convexity), completeness of R (least upper bounds, Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem), and sequences in R and Rⁿ.
